headline: What’s Next for Goldman Sachs and Other Banks After Acing Fed's Stress Test
summary: All 22 banks in this year’s stress test had enough capital to absorb more than $550 billion in losses during a severe downturn, and still continue lending, the Fed said late Friday.  Under the Fed's hypothetical recession scenario, banks’ common equity Tier 1 capital ratio—a key measure of balance-sheet strength which calculates capital against assets—declined 1.8 percentage points.  Goldman Sachs is set to see the largest easing of capital requirements, said Barclays analysts.

headline: Will Americans See Relief From Rising Insurance Costs? Not Anytime Soon.
summary: Consumers across the U.S. continue to feel the pinch of rising auto and homeowner’s insurance costs.  Median auto and home insurance payments rose 6% during the 12 months that ended May 31, according to a new report from the  Bank of America Institute  which used internal bank customer data to track payments.  The unit of Bank of America says the pace of increase is moderating but notes the cost has taken a toll on consumers.

headline: US CFPB ends monitoring of BofA over mortgage reporting case
summary: The move ends the consumer watchdog's monitoring of the lender more than three years ahead of schedule.  In November 2023, BofA had agreed to pay a $12 million fine to settle CFPB charges that it routinely submitted inaccurate information about mortgage applicants to the federal government, in violation of longstanding laws.  The bank neither admitted nor denied wrongdoing, but as part of the settlement, the CFPB was set to oversee the issue for five years unless it chose to end its oversight early.

headline: How Stablecoins Can Be Destabilizing
summary: Stablecoins’ going mainstream wouldn’t take all of banks’ deposits away.  The Senate looks set to soon pass the so-called Genius Act, which will set guidelines for issuers of stablecoins—digital tokens that are fully backed by fiat currencies such as dollars.  One big debate over the wisdom of giving stablecoins a regulatory framework centers around how they would affect the current banking system if they were to hugely expand in size.

David Larsen will join the Swiss bank in September, to focus on application software, according to the memo by Laurence Braham, the global co-head of technology for UBS' investment bank.  "David and Rob collectively bring over 30 years of banking experience and have advised clients on numerous high-profile M&A and capital markets transactions," Braham wrote.
